title: ASIC releases 2025–26 industry levy
full_text:
ASIC has confirmed the estimated industry funding levy for the 2025–26 financial year, and how much super trustees can expect to pay.
According to its annual Cost Recovery Implementation Statement (CRIS), ASIC said the total for the investment management, superannuation, and related services stood at $74.4 million.
This is up from $63.7 million in 2024-25.
For superannuation trustees specifically, there will be a minimum levy of $18,000 plus $8.65 per $1 million of assets above the $250 million threshold.
This brings the 2025-26 estimated costs to $25 million, split across supervision, surveillance, and enforcement.
Unlike several other sectors, the figure reduced for superannuation trustees, down from total costs of $26.6 million in 2024-25.
Source: ASIC, July 2026
For responsible entities, the estimated costs are $35 million with a minimum levy of $7,000 plus $16.52 per $1 million of assets above $10 million threshold.
The figures for responsible entities are 29 per cent higher than 2024-25 when the total cost was $27.1 million.
For wholesale trustees, these have estimated costs of $8.9 million with a minimum levy of $1,000 plus $6.83 per $1 million of adjusted total assets. Like responsible entities, wholesale trustees saw an increase of 48 per cent as 2024-25 estimated total costs were $6 million.
author: Laura Dew
heading: Superannuation and Investments
page_title: ASIC releases 2025–26 industry levy - Super Review
